canto_title: The Center of the Earth and the Encounter with Lucifer

scenes.summary: Dante and Virgil move through a thick fog that obscures the landscape.
The narrator describes the various positions of the souls lost in the darkness.
Virgil stops Dante to present the terrifying sight of the City of Dis.
Dante describes his profound state of shock and loss of words.
The massive, once-beautiful figure of the Emperor of Pain emerges from the ice.
A detailed description of Lucifer's three faces, six wings, and the freezing winds they create.
The sight of Lucifer using his teeth to tear apart sinners like morsels of food.
Virgil identifies the three specific souls being eaten: Judas, Brutus, and Cassio.
Virgil leads Dante down the hairy, frozen body of Lucifer.
The pair reaches the point where the weight of the world shifts, causing Dante to fear a fall.
Virgil finds a stable place to sit Dante on the edge of a rock.

scenes.scene_name: The Sight of the Upside-Down Legs
scenes.summary: Dante looks back and sees the terrifying sight of Lucifer’s legs pointing upward.
The narrator describes the arduous and dark terrain they must now traverse.
Dante asks Virgil to explain the strange physics and the sudden change in time.
Virgil explains that they have passed the center of the earth and are now in the opposite hemisphere.
Virgil explains the cosmic movement of the earth and the displacement of the air.
The travelers hear a small stream nearby, signaling their proximity to the exit.
The pair climbs out of the abyss and emerges into the light to see the stars once again.
